mozambican-prawns.blurb: Mozambican coastal treasure — prawns grilled with peri-peri and garlic butter until charred and succulent.

prov.popular_in: Mozambique and South African coastal restaurants
prov.famous_for: The combination of peri-peri heat with rich garlic butter
prov.consists_of: Large prawns (LM prawns) marinated in peri-peri, grilled or pan-fried and basted with garlic butter until charred and cooked
prov.name_origin: From Mozambique's coastal cuisine; 'LM' refers to large Mozambican prawns
prov.why_loved: Succulent prawns with spicy, buttery flavours — the taste of the Indian Ocean
prov.method_detail: Fire/stovetop — prawns marinated in peri-peri, grilled over coals or pan-fried 3–4 min per side, basted with garlic butter until charred

mozambican-prawns.pairing: A crisp dry white wine or cold lager; serve with bread and peri-peri sauce.

fish-and-chips.blurb: Britain's national dish — beer-battered cod or haddock, thick-cut chips, mushy peas and tartare sauce.

prov.popular_in: United Kingdom, Australia, New Zealand; chip shops worldwide
prov.famous_for: The perfect marriage of crispy battered fish and fluffy chips, wrapped in paper with vinegar
prov.consists_of: White fish fillets in light beer batter, deep-fried until golden, served with thick-cut chips and mushy peas
prov.name_origin: British working-class dish from 19th-century England; Jewish and French influences shaped the batter
prov.why_loved: Crispy batter, flaky fish, fluffy chips and a splash of vinegar — comfort food perfection
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — fish dipped in seasoned flour, then beer batter, fried 5–6 min at 180°C until golden; chips parboiled then fried

fish-and-chips.pairing: A cold lager or cup of tea; serve with mushy peas and tartare sauce.

shepherds-pie.blurb: Britain's comfort classic — slow-cooked lamb in rich gravy topped with creamy mashed potato, baked until golden.

prov.popular_in: British and Irish households, especially in winter
prov.famous_for: The golden mashed potato crust that protects the rich lamb filling beneath
prov.consists_of: Slow-cooked lamb mince with onions, carrots and peas in gravy, topped with mashed potato and baked
prov.name_origin: 18th-century British/Scottish dish; 'shepherd's' implies lamb (lamb shepherds), 'cottage pie' uses beef
prov.why_loved: Humble, filling and deeply satisfying — the ultimate home-cooked comfort food
prov.method_detail: Oven — lamb mince browned with vegetables, simmered in gravy 20–30 min, topped with mashed potato, baked 25–30 min at 200°C until golden

shepherds-pie.pairing: A robust red wine or a dark ale; serve with buttered greens.

cottage-pie.blurb: Shepherd's pie's beef cousin — rich beef mince in gravy topped with creamy mashed potato and baked golden.

prov.popular_in: British households and pubs
prov.famous_for: The beef version of the classic potato-topped pie, perfect for feeding families
prov.consists_of: Beef mince browned with onions, carrots and celery in gravy, topped with mashed potato and baked
prov.name_origin: 'Cottage pie' dates to the 18th century; the term pre-dates 'shepherd's pie' and refers to the humble cottage dwelling
prov.why_loved: Hearty, economical and deeply comforting — the family favourite that never disappoints
prov.method_detail: Oven — beef mince browned with vegetables, simmered in gravy 20 min, topped with mashed potato, baked 20–25 min at 200°C until golden

cottage-pie.pairing: A robust red wine or stout; serve with green beans.

chicken-pot-pie.blurb: American comfort food — tender chicken and vegetables in creamy sauce, topped with flaky pastry and baked golden.

prov.popular_in: American households and diners, especially in the Midwest and Northeast
prov.famous_for: The flaky pastry crust that breaks into creamy chicken-filled comfort with every forkful
prov.consists_of: Poached chicken pieces with carrots, celery and peas in creamy white sauce, topped with pastry and baked
prov.name_origin: American adaptation of British meat pies; 'pot pie' refers to the deep dish it's baked in
prov.why_loved: Flaky pastry, creamy sauce and tender chicken — the definition of comfort food
prov.method_detail: Oven — chicken poached, vegetables sautéed, sauce made, filled into pie dish, topped with pastry, baked 30–35 min at 200°C until golden

chicken-pot-pie.pairing: A buttery Chardonnay or a cold lager; serve with green salad.

chicken-noodle-soup.blurb: The universal cure — tender chicken, egg noodles and vegetables in golden broth, the medicine that tastes like love.

prov.popular_in: American households and delis worldwide; Jewish penicillin
prov.famous_for: Being the ultimate comfort food for colds, cold days and homesickness
prov.consists_of: Chicken pieces simmered with onions, carrots, celery and egg noodles in seasoned broth
prov.name_origin: Eastern European Jewish-American dish; modern versions include egg noodles, not matzo balls
prov.why_loved: Soothing, nourishing and deeply nostalgic — the soup that fixes everything
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — chicken browned, vegetables added, broth simmered 45–60 min, noodles added 5–6 min, finished with parsley

chicken-noodle-soup.pairing: Saltine crackers or crusty bread; a cup of tea.

cream-of-tomato-soup.blurb: Childhood comfort — tomatoes slow-cooked into velvety cream soup, the perfect partner for grilled cheese.

prov.popular_in: American households and diners; Campbell's made it famous
prov.famous_for: Being the classic partner to grilled cheese sandwiches; Campbell's condensed version defined it
prov.consists_of: Tomatoes slow-cooked with onions and garlic, puréed with cream into velvety soup
prov.name_origin: American invention; Campbell's condensed tomato soup launched in 1897 made it a staple
prov.why_loved: Sweet, tangy and creamy — the soup that tastes like childhood
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — tomatoes slow-cooked with onions and garlic 30–40 min, puréed, cream added, heated through

cream-of-tomato-soup.pairing: Grilled cheese sandwich and saltine crackers.

garlic-bread.blurb: The ultimate pizza side — crusty bread slathered in garlic butter and grilled until golden and fragrant.

prov.popular_in: Pizza restaurants and Italian-American households worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being the irresistible side that outsells pizza itself in many restaurants
prov.consists_of: Baguette or Italian bread sliced, buttered with garlic-parsley butter, grilled until golden
prov.name_origin: Italian-American invention; traditional Italian meals offer plain bread, but America added garlic butter
prov.why_loved: Crisp, buttery and garlicky — the carb that makes every meal better
prov.method_detail: Oven — bread sliced, butter mixed with garlic and parsley, spread on bread, grilled 8–10 min at 200°C until golden

garlic-bread.pairing: Serve alongside pizza, pasta or salad; pairs with red or white wine.

bruschetta.blurb: Tuscan's grilled bread — crusty bread rubbed with garlic, topped with fresh tomatoes, basil and olive oil.

prov.popular_in: Italian households and restaurants worldwide
prov.famous_for: The combination of charred bread, juicy tomatoes and fresh basil that defines Italian summer
prov.consists_of: Grilled bread rubbed with garlic, topped with diced tomatoes, basil, olive oil and salt
prov.name_origin: From 'bruscare' (to toast over coals); central Italian peasant food dating to ancient Rome
prov.why_loved: Simple, fresh and bursting with flavour — the perfect starter or snack
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — bread grilled until charred, rubbed with garlic, topped with tomato-basil mixture, drizzled with olive oil

bruschetta.pairing: A crisp dry white wine; serve as a starter or with antipasti.

caprese-salad.blurb: Capri's colours — ripe tomatoes, fresh mozzarella and basil drizzled with olive oil, the Italian flag on a plate.

prov.popular_in: Italian restaurants and summer tables worldwide
prov.famous_for: The stunning red-white-green presentation that mirrors the Italian flag
prov.consists_of: Sliced ripe tomatoes and fresh mozzarella layered with basil, dressed with olive oil and balsamic
prov.name_origin: From Capri island; simple salad that celebrates Italy's finest ingredients
prov.why_loved: Fresh, elegant and let the ingredients shine — summer on a plate
prov.method_detail: No-cook — tomatoes and mozzarella sliced, layered with basil, drizzled with olive oil and balsamic, served immediately

caprese-salad.pairing: A crisp Pinot Grigio or dry Rosé; serve with crusty bread.

caesar-salad.blurb: Tijuana's masterpiece — crisp cos lettuce, garlicky croutons, Parmesan and creamy Caesar dressing.

prov.popular_in: Restaurants and households worldwide; invented in Tijuana, Mexico
prov.famous_for: The creamy, garlicky dressing with anchovy that defines the salad
prov.consists_of: Crisp cos lettuce tossed with garlicky croutons, shaved Parmesan and creamy Caesar dressing
prov.name_origin: Created by Caesar Cardini in 1924 in Tijuana, Mexico; Italian-Mexican-American fusion
prov.why_loved: Crisp, creamy and savoury — the salad that feels like a meal
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — croutons fried in garlic oil, dressing made with egg, anchovy, garlic and lemon, lettuce tossed and topped

caesar-salad.pairing: A crisp dry white wine or add grilled chicken for a main.

nachos.blurb: Tex-Mex's sharing classic — tortilla chips buried under melted cheese, jalapeños, guacamole and sour cream.

prov.popular_in: Tex-Mex restaurants, sports bars and parties worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being the ultimate party food — customizable, shareable and impossible to stop eating
prov.consists_of: Tortilla chips topped with melted cheese, jalapeños and various toppings, baked until bubbly
prov.name_origin: Invented in 1943 in Piedras Negras, Mexico; Ignacio 'Nacho' Anaya created it for American wives of soldiers
prov.why_loved: Crunchy, cheesy and moreish — the snack that brings people together
prov.method_detail: Oven — tortilla chips layered with cheese and jalapeños, baked 5–10 min at 200°C until melted, topped with guacamole and sour cream

nachos.pairing: Ice-cold beer or margaritas; serve as a starter or party food.

chicken-wings.blurb: Buffalo's gift — crispy chicken wings tossed in spicy hot sauce, served with blue cheese dip and celery.

prov.popular_in: American sports bars and parties worldwide; Buffalo's claim to fame
prov.famous_for: The spicy Buffalo wing that revolutionised bar food in 1964
prov.consists_of: Chicken wings deep-fried or baked until crispy, tossed in spicy buttered hot sauce
prov.name_origin: Invented at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York, in 1964 by Teressa Bellissimo
prov.why_loved: Crispy, spicy and addictive — the perfect party food
prov.method_detail: Oven/stovetop — wings fried or baked 35–45 min until crispy, tossed in hot sauce and butter, served with blue cheese dip

chicken-wings.pairing: Ice-cold beer; serve with blue cheese dip and celery sticks.

pulled-pork.blurb: Carolina's BBQ classic — pork shoulder slow-cooked until meltingly tender, shredded and piled into buns.

prov.popular_in: American South, especially North Carolina; BBQ joints worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being tender enough to pull apart with two forks, with a smoky bark and juicy interior
prov.consists_of: Pork shoulder rubbed with spices, slow-cooked until tender, shredded and sauced
prov.name_origin: Traditional Southern American BBQ; slow-cooking tough cuts makes them tender
prov.why_loved: Smoky, tender and piled high — the BBQ that feeds a crowd
prov.method_detail: Oven — pork shoulder rubbed with spices, slow-cooked 3–4 hours at 150°C until tender, shredded, tossed in BBQ sauce

pulled-pork.pairing: A cold lager or sweet tea; serve with coleslaw and baked beans.

meatloaf.blurb: Comfort on a plate — seasoned beef mince loaf baked with a tangy glaze, served with mashed potato.

prov.popular_in: American and European households, especially in winter
prov.famous_for: Being the ultimate budget-friendly comfort food that feeds a family for days
prov.consists_of: Beef mince mixed with breadcrumbs, eggs and seasonings, shaped into a loaf, baked with glaze
prov.name_origin: Ancient dish; American meatloaf became popular during the Depression as economical meat-stretching
prov.why_loved: Humble, savoury and deeply satisfying — home cooking at its finest
prov.method_detail: Oven — mince mixed with breadcrumbs, onions and eggs, shaped into loaf, baked 1 hour at 180°C, glazed last 15 min, rested 10 min

meatloaf.pairing: Ketchup or gravy; serve with mashed potato and green beans.

roast-beef.blurb: Sunday's centerpiece — seasoned beef roast cooked to pink perfection, served with Yorkshire puddings and gravy.

prov.popular_in: British Sunday roasts and holiday tables worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being the centerpiece of the traditional British Sunday roast
prov.consists_of: Beef rib or sirloin seasoned and roasted to medium-rare, served with Yorkshire puddings and horseradish
prov.name_origin: Traditional British Sunday dish; Yorkshire pudding originally served as a filler with the gravy
prov.why_loved: Pink, tender and slicing perfectly at the table — celebration food at its finest
prov.method_detail: Oven — beef seared, roasted at 200°C 15 min, reduced to 180°C 1–1.5 hours until 55°C internal, rested 20 min

roast-beef.pairing: A robust red wine (Cabernet Sauvignon or Shiraz); serve with Yorkshire puddings and horseradish.

sausage-rolls.blurb: Britain's savoury pastry — seasoned sausage meat wrapped in flaky puff pastry and baked golden.

prov.popular_in: British bakeries, parties and picnics; Australian butcher shops also claim them
prov.famous_for: Being the ultimate party food — handheld, savoury and impossible to stop at one
prov.consists_of: Seasoned sausage meat wrapped in puff pastry, egg-washed and baked until golden
prov.name_origin: British invention; became popular in the 19th century as a portable snack
prov.why_loved: Flaky pastry, savoury sausage — the perfect handheld snack
prov.method_detail: Oven — sausage meat seasoned, wrapped in puff pastry, egg-washed, baked 20–25 min at 200°C until golden

sausage-rolls.pairing: Mustard or tomato sauce; a cold beer or cider.

frittata.blurb: Italy's open-face omelette — eggs baked with vegetables, cheese and herbs, served warm or at room temperature.

prov.popular_in: Italian households and brunch menus worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being the versatile egg dish that works for breakfast, lunch or dinner
prov.consists_of: Eggs beaten with vegetables, cheese and herbs, cooked on stovetop then finished under the grill
prov.name_origin: Italian for 'fried'; differs from quiche in having no pastry crust
prov.why_loved: Simple, versatile and delicious — eggs elevated to a meal
prov.method_detail: Stovetop/oven — vegetables sautéed, eggs poured over, cooked on stovetop until set, finished under grill 5–8 min

frittata.pairing: Green salad and crusty bread; a dry white wine or prosecco.

french-toast.blurb: Brunch's classic — bread dipped in eggy custard, fried until golden, served with maple syrup and berries.

prov.popular_in: American brunch spots and households worldwide
prov.famous_for: Being the sweet brunch staple that transforms stale bread into luxury
prov.consists_of: Bread slices dipped in egg-milk mixture, fried until golden, served with syrup and butter
prov.name_origin: Not French — medieval European recipe called 'pain perdu' (lost bread) to use stale bread
prov.why_loved: Crisp outside, custard-soft inside — the ultimate indulgent breakfast
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — bread dipped in egg-milk-vanilla mixture, fried in butter 2–3 min per side until golden

french-toast.pairing: Crispy bacon or fresh berries; coffee or orange juice.

waffles.blurb: Brunch's crisp classic — batter cooked in a patterned iron, served with butter, syrup and whipped cream.

prov.popular_in: Brunch spots worldwide; Belgian waffles made them famous
prov.famous_for: The crisp exterior and fluffy interior with deep pockets for syrup
prov.consists_of: Egg-butter-milk batter cooked in a waffle iron until crisp, served with sweet toppings
prov.name_origin: Belgian invention; waffle irons date to the Middle Ages, modern waffles popularised at the 1964 World's Fair
prov.why_loved: Crisp, fluffy and customisable — breakfast that feels like dessert
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — batter made, waffle iron heated, batter cooked 3–5 min until crisp, served immediately with toppings

waffles.pairing: Maple syrup, berries and whipped cream; coffee or hot chocolate.

ceviche-classico.blurb: Peru's national dish — raw white fish 'cooked' in lime juice with red onion, coriander and a hint of chilli.

prov.popular_in: Peru and across Latin America; now global in cevicherías
prov.famous_for: The citrus-cure technique that denatures fish without heat, creating fresh, bright flavour
prov.consists_of: Raw white fish cubes marinated in lime juice with red onion, coriander, chilli and ginger, served chilled
prov.name_origin: From Quechua 'siwichi' (fresh fish); Moorish citrus-cure technique brought by Spanish colonists
prov.why_loved: The clean bright flavours, silky texture and freshness make it the ultimate warm-weather starter
prov.method_detail: Raw — fish cubed and marinated in lime juice 15–20 min until opaque, folded with onion, coriander, chilli, served chilled

ceviche-classico.pairing: A crisp Peruvian beer or sauvignon blanc; serve with sweet potato and corn.

xiaolongbao.blurb: Shanghai's legendary soup dumplings — thin-skinned dumplings filled with pork and rich, gelatinous broth.

prov.popular_in: Shanghai and Chinese dim sum houses worldwide
prov.famous_for: The explosive soup inside each delicate dumpling — the ultimate umami surprise
prov.consists_of: Thin wheat-flour wrappers filled with pork mince and pork aspic (gelatinised broth), steamed until the aspic melts into soup
prov.name_origin: Xiaolongbao means 'little basket bun' — named after the bamboo steamer baskets they're cooked in
prov.why_loved: The ritual of biting, sipping the broth, then eating the dumpling — interactive, luxurious, deeply satisfying
prov.method_detail: Steam — pork aspic made from simmered skin and bones, minced pork mixed with aspic, wrapped in thin dough, steamed 10–12 min

xiaolongbao.pairing: Black vinegar with ginger; serve with chilli oil and Chinese tea.

okonomiyaki.blurb: Osaka's soul food — a savoury cabbage pancake loaded with pork belly, seafood and topped with sauces and mayo.

prov.popular_in: Osaka and Hiroshima; Japanese izakayas worldwide
prov.famous_for: The crispy exterior, tender cabbage centre, and the signature sweet-savoury toppings
prov.consists_of: A batter of flour, eggs and water mixed with shredded cabbage, grilled with pork belly or seafood, topped with sweet sauce, mayonnaise and bonito
prov.name_origin: Okonomiyaki means 'grilled how you like' — okonomi (preference) + yaki (grill)
prov.why_loved: Customisable, comforting, with irresistible texture contrasts and addictive toppings
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — batter mixed with cabbage, pan-fried with pork belly until golden, flipped, finished with sauce, mayo and bonito flakes

okonomiyaki.pairing: Cold Japanese beer; serve with miso soup and pickles.

yakitori.blurb: Japanese charcoal-grilled chicken skewers — basted with tare sauce until glazed and smoky.

prov.popular_in: Japanese izakayas and street stalls; now global in Japanese restaurants
prov.famous_for: The smoky charcoal flavour and the tare-glazed, lacquered finish on each skewer
prov.consists_of: Chicken parts (thigh, skin, liver) skewered and grilled over binchotan charcoal, basted repeatedly with tare (soy-mirin-sake sauce)
prov.name_origin: Yakitori means 'grilled bird' — yaki (grill) + tori (bird); originated as street food in the Meiji era
prov.why_loved: The smoky-sweet glaze, juicy meat, and the ritual of eating skewer after skewer with beer
prov.method_detail: Grill — chicken skewered, grilled over hot charcoal at 180–200°C, basted with tare every 2–3 min until glazed, 10–12 min total

yakitori.pairing: Cold Japanese beer; serve with cabbage salad and rice.

takoyaki.blurb: Osaka's famous octopus balls — crispy outside, molten inside, filled with tender octopus and green onion.

prov.popular_in: Osaka street stalls and Japanese festivals worldwide
prov.famous_for: The dramatic contrast between the crispy shell and creamy, molten centre with octopus chunks
prov.consists_of: A batter-based mixture cooked in specialised half-spherical moulds, filled with octopus, green onion and pickled ginger, brushed with sauce and mayo
prov.name_origin: Takoyaki means 'grilled octopus' — tako (octopus) + yaki (grill); invented in Osaka in 1935
prov.why_loved: Fun to eat, incredible texture, and the quintessential Japanese street food experience
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — batter poured into hot moulded half-spheres, octopus added, cooked 3–4 min, turned with skewer to form balls, finished 2–3 min, sauced

takoyaki.pairing: Cold beer; serve with Japanese mustard and pickled vegetables.

banh-xeo.blurb: Vietnam's sizzling pancake — a turmeric-yellow crêpe filled with pork, prawns and bean sprouts.

prov.popular_in: Central Vietnam and Vietnamese homes worldwide
prov.famous_for: The dramatic sizzle when batter hits the hot pan — bánh xèo means 'sizzling cake'
prov.consists_of: A rice flour and turmeric batter cooked until crispy, filled with pork, prawns and bean sprouts, served with lettuce and herbs for wrapping
prov.name_origin: Bánh xèo literally means 'sizzling cake' — the sound of batter hitting the hot pan
prov.why_loved: Interactive DIY wrapping, texture contrasts and fresh herb-y balance against rich crispy pancake
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — batter ladled into hot oiled pan, pork and prawns added, folded over bean sprouts, cooked until crispy, 4–5 min per side

banh-xeo.pairing: Cold beer; serve with nuoc cham dipping sauce and fresh herbs.

laarb-mince.blurb: The national dish of Laos — fragrant minced meat salad with lime, chilli, mint and roasted rice powder.

prov.popular_in: Laos and Thailand's Isan region; now in Thai restaurants globally
prov.famous_for: The unique texture from khao khua (roasted rice powder) and the hot-lime-herb balance
prov.consists_of: Minced meat stir-fried with aromatics, seasoned with fish sauce and lime, tossed with mint, coriander and roasted rice powder, served at room temperature
prov.name_origin: Laarb comes from Lao; means 'meat salad' — a Lao invention that spread to Isan
prov.why_loved: Intensely flavoured, textural, and the perfect balance of hot, sour, salty and herbal — the ultimate Southeast Asian salad
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — mince stir-fried with galangal and garlic until dry, seasoned with fish sauce and lime juice, tossed with herbs and khao khua, served room temperature

laarb-mince.pairing: Sticky rice; serve with fresh vegetables and cold beer.

nasi-lemak.blurb: Malaysia's beloved breakfast — coconut rice served with sambal, fried anchovies, egg and cucumber.

prov.popular_in: Malaysia and Singapore; the national dish of Malaysia
prov.famous_for: The fragrant coconut milk rice and the sambal — spicy, sweet and savoury
prov.consists_of: Rice cooked in coconut milk and pandan leaves, served with sambal, crispy fried anchovies (ikan bilis), roasted peanuts, egg and cucumber
prov.name_origin: Nasi lemak means 'rich/fat rice' — from the coconut milk that makes it luxurious
prov.why_loved: The ultimate comfort breakfast — creamy rice, fiery sambal, crunchy anchovies and cooling cucumber
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — rice cooked in coconut milk with pandan leaf, sambal made separately, assembled with ikan bilis, peanuts, egg and cucumber

nasi-lemak.pairing: Malaysian teh tarik or coffee; serve with fried chicken or rendang.

laksa-curry.blurb: Penang's iconic noodle soup — rice noodles in a rich, coconut-curry broth with tofu, prawns and fish balls.

prov.popular_in: Penang and Malaysia; now globally in Malaysian restaurants
prov.famous_for: The complex curry-coconut broth spiced with torch ginger and makrut lime
prov.consists_of: Rice vermicelli in a spicy coconut curry broth with tofu puffs, prawns, fish balls, cockles and cucumber, topped with herbs
prov.name_origin: Laksa comes from Sanskrit 'laksha' (many) — referring to the many ingredients; evolved from Chinese noodle soups in Malay ports
prov.why_loved: Rich coconut curry, slippery noodles and fresh herbs — the ultimate comfort noodle soup
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — rempah spice paste fried, coconut milk and stock added, simmered 20 min, noodles added, assembled with toppings and herbs

laksa-curry.pairing: Cold beer or iced tea; serve with sambal on the side.

taiwanese-beef-noodle-soup.blurb: Taiwan's national dish — beef shank braised in soy-star anise broth with thick wheat noodles and pickled mustard greens.

prov.popular_in: Taiwan and Taiwanese restaurants worldwide
prov.famous_for: The tender, falling-apart beef and the aromatic soy-star anise broth
prov.consists_of: Beef shank braised in soy sauce, star anise, ginger and garlic until tender, served in the braising liquid with thick wheat noodles and pickled mustard greens
prov.name_origin: Created by post-1949 Mainland Chinese immigrants in Taiwan; evolved from northern Chinese braised beef dishes
prov.why_loved: Deep umami broth, meltingly tender beef and the sour crunch of pickled greens against chewy noodles
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — beef blanched, braised in soy, star anise, ginger, garlic, rock sugar 90 min until tender, served with noodles and pickled mustard greens

taiwanese-beef-noodle-soup.pairing: Cold Taiwanese beer; serve with pickled vegetables.

jianbing.blurb: Northern China's breakfast crêpe — a thin savoury crêpe with egg, spring onion, crispy wonton and hoisin sauce.

prov.popular_in: Northern China street stalls; now in Chinese breakfast spots worldwide
prov.famous_for: The addictive texture contrasts — silky crêpe, crispy wonton, fresh herbs — all rolled into one
prov.consists_of: A thin mung bean-flour crêpe cooked on a griddle, topped with egg, spring onion, crispy wonton skin, brushed with chilli and hoisin sauces, folded up
prov.name_origin: Jianbing means 'fried pancake' — from Tianjin and Shandong; a common Northern Chinese street breakfast since the Qing dynasty
prov.why_loved: Quick, satisfying, portable and textural — the ultimate Chinese breakfast on the go
prov.method_detail: Stovetop — batter ladled onto hot griddle, egg cracked and spread, topped with spring onion and crispy wonton, brushed with sauces, folded and rolled

jianbing.pairing: Soy milk; serve with pickled vegetables.

khachapuri.blurb: Georgia's cheese-filled bread boat — yeasted dough filled with melted cheese and topped with a runny egg.

prov.popular_in: Georgia and across the Caucasus; now in Eastern European restaurants worldwide
prov.famous_for: The dramatic boat shape and the ritual of tearing off bread crusts to dip into the cheese and egg
prov.consists_of: Yeasted dough shaped into a boat, filled with a mix of mozzarella and feta cheese, baked until golden, topped with an egg and butter
prov.name_origin: Khachapuri comes from Georgian 'khacho' (curd cheese) + 'puri' (bread); dates to the medieval period
prov.why_loved: The combination of crisp bread, molten cheese and runny yolk is pure comfort — interactive and indulgent
prov.method_detail: Oven — dough proofed 1 hour, shaped into boats, filled with cheese mix, baked at 200°C 15–18 min until golden, topped with egg, baked 3–4 min more

khachapuri.pairing: Georgian red wine; serve with fresh herbs and tomato salad.
